concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains two distinct meaningful images on the first page: the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina at the top, and the NC Department of Environmental Quality logo at the bottom. While the DEQ logo is correctly retained as an element in the HTML output, the State Seal is entirely missing as an image and has been improperly replaced with a text placeholder paragraph. Since the State Seal is a meaningful organizational logo that identifies the origin of the document, its
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Great Seal of the State of North Carolina positioned at the top center of the page.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (9): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=9, pages_affected=9/10. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

The source document contains a large 7-column table that spans multiple pages ('North Carolina Aquatic Weed Control Program'). While the first few pages of this table (extracted on pages 4 and 8 of the JSON) correctly maintain the 7-column structure, the continuations extracted on JSON pages 6 and 9 incorrectly split the funding amounts into separate columns for the dollar sign and the numerical value. This changes the structure from 7 columns to 9 columns on these pages, breaking column alignme
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The original table has 7 columns (Project Type, Project sub-type, Cooperator, Project, Proposed State Funding Amount, Proposed Cooperator Funding Amount, Description).
concerns.output_quote: The extracted table on Page 6 creates 9 columns. For example, Row 0 splits the amounts: column 4 is '$', column 5 is '800.00', column 6 is '$', and column 7 is '800.00'.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['mailto:Kevin.Hart@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
